name: Do All Visual Tokens Matter Equally? Object-Evidence Preserving Token Merging for Vision-Language Retrieval
summary: arXiv:2607.04605v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Multi-vector vision-language retrieval preserves fine-grained visual evidence through maximum-similarity late interaction, but dense image-...
description: arXiv:2607.04605v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Multi-vector vision-language retrieval preserves fine-grained visual evidence through maximum-similarity late interaction, but dense image-side tokens make storage and scoring expensive. Existing token compression methods reduce this cost, yet they can remove or collapse object- and region-level evidence that future query tokens may need...
author: Suhyeong Park, Junha Jung, Jungwoo Park, Jaewoo Kang
